description: Walking skeleton: bot user + webhook model + POST endpoint + real-time message delivery

steps.name: Add is_bot to User schema + BOT badge display
steps.description: Add is_bot boolean to User schema with migration. Update serialize_sender in ChannelServer and CatchupServer to include is_bot. Add BOT badge to message_bubble component. Add bot_changeset/2 to User.
acceptance_criteria: Migration adds is_bot boolean column (default false) to users table
User schema has is_bot field
User.bot_changeset/2 creates a bot user (is_bot: true, no password)
ChannelServer.serialize_sender/1 includes is_bot in output
CatchupServer.serialize_sender/1 includes is_bot in output
message_bubble displays [BOT] badge when sender.is_bot is true
Regular users do not show BOT badge

steps.name: Webhook schema + Integrations.Webhooks context
steps.description: Create Webhook Ecto schema, migration, and Integrations.Webhooks context module. Webhook creation uses Ecto.Multi to atomically: generate token, hash it, create bot user, resolve channel, subscribe bot to channel, insert webhook record.
acceptance_criteria: Migration creates webhooks table with token_hash, name, channel_id, bot_user_id, inserted_at
Webhook schema with token_hash (unique index), name, belongs_to channel and bot_user
Webhooks.create_webhook/1 generates token, hashes with SHA-256, creates bot user, subscribes to channel, inserts webhook — all in Ecto.Multi transaction
Webhooks.get_by_token_hash/1 looks up webhook by hashed token (preloads channel and bot_user)
create_webhook rejects private channels with {:error, :private_channel_not_supported}
Token is returned plaintext on creation only (not stored)
All operations are atomic — partial creation is impossible

steps.name: WebhookController + router + payload enforcement
steps.description: Create POST /api/webhooks/:token endpoint. Validates token, checks feature flag, validates payload (text required, 16KB limit), verifies channel exists, sends message via Messaging.send_message. Route is outside all auth pipelines.
acceptance_criteria: POST /api/webhooks/:token route exists outside auth pipelines
Invalid token returns 401 with JSON error
Missing/empty text field returns 400 with JSON error
Payload over 16KB returns 413
Deleted/missing channel returns 404
Valid request sends message through ChannelServer pipeline and returns 200
Message appears in real-time via PubSub to connected LiveView clients
Feature flag :incoming_webhooks gates the endpoint (returns 404 when disabled)

steps.name: Integration test + CI webhook setup
steps.description: End-to-end test: create webhook, POST to it, verify message appears in channel with BOT badge. Update ci-deploy.yml to POST deploy notifications to Slackex webhook.
acceptance_criteria: Integration test: create_webhook → POST /api/webhooks/:token → message in DB with bot sender
Integration test: invalid token → 401
Integration test: missing text → 400
Integration test: feature flag disabled → 404
ci-deploy.yml has Slackex webhook POST step (alongside existing Discord)
